evals.prompt: I want to predict the structure of a short peptide using OpenFold3. The sequence is MKTVRQERLKSIVRGPKRAKELMSI. Use the hosted NVIDIA API with my NGC_API_KEY already set in environment.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that calls the hosted OpenFold3 endpoint with Bearer auth, constructs an inputs payload with the peptide sequence as a protein molecule type including a minimal MSA, saves the returned structure to a PDB or CIF file, and prints the confidence scores.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted OpenFold3 endpoint URL
Sets Authorization header with Bearer token from NGC_API_KEY
Payload uses 'inputs' array containing molecule objects
assertions.check: Script payload contains 'inputs' key with an array/list containing 'molecules'

assertions.description: Molecule object sets type to 'protein'
assertions.check: Script contains 'type' and 'protein' in the molecule specification

assertions.description: MSA field is provided with nested alignment structure
assertions.check: Script contains 'msa' with an alignment string starting with '>query'

assertions.description: Saves the returned structure to a file and prints confidence scores
assertions.check: Script writes structure content to a file and references 'confidence_score' or 'structures_with_scores' from the response

evals.prompt: I want to predict a protein-ligand complex structure using OpenFold3. My protein sequence is MTEYKLVVVGACGVGKSALTIQLIQNHFVDEYDPTIEDSYRKQVVID and I want to co-fold it with ATP (CCD code: ATP). Use the hosted API.
evals.expected_output: A Python script with two molecules in the payload — a protein with sequence and MSA, and a ligand using ccd_codes set to ATP — calling the hosted endpoint, saving structure output, and printing pLDDT and confidence scores.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
Payload includes both a protein molecule and a ligand molecule
assertions.check: Script payload contains both 'type' set to 'protein' and 'type' set to 'ligand' in two separate molecule objects

assertions.description: Ligand uses ccd_codes field set to ATP
The provided protein sequence appears in the payload
Prints or displays confidence score and pLDDT from response
assertions.check: Script references 'confidence_score' and 'complex_plddt_score' or 'plddt' from the response

assertions.description: Saves the predicted structure to a PDB or CIF file
assertions.check: Script writes structure content to a file with .pdb or .cif extension

evals.prompt: Help me set up the OpenFold3 NIM locally with Docker. I have an A100 80GB GPU and NGC_API_KEY is set. After setup, predict the structure of this protein: ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY.
evals.expected_output: Docker setup commands using shell env first and optional repo-root .env overrides, requiring NGC_API_KEY or NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback plus LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, running with --gpus 'device=0', --shm-size=16g, and the correct cache mount path, then a health check loop and no-auth prediction script targeting localhost:8000.

assertions.description: Includes docker login command for nvcr.io with oauthtoken
References the correct OpenFold3 container image
Uses single GPU device specification
Includes --shm-size flag (required for this NIM)
Local setup uses the repo env contract and LOCAL_NIM_CACHE
assertions.check: Output sources repo-root .env only if present, supports NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback to NGC_API_KEY, requires LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, and mounts LOCAL_NIM_CACHE to /opt/nim/.cache

assertions.description: Prediction script targets localhost:8000 without /v1/ prefix

evals.prompt: I have a protein-DNA complex I want to predict with OpenFold3 via the hosted API. Protein chain A: MTEYKLVVVGACGVGKSALTIQLIQNHFVDE. DNA chain B: ATCGATCGATCG (sense strand), DNA chain C: CGATCGATCGAT (antisense strand). I want 2 diffusion samples and output in CIF format.
evals.expected_output: A Python script with three molecules in the payload (protein + two DNA chains), diffusion_samples set to 2, output_format set to 'cif', using the hosted endpoint. Saves and names the returned CIF files and prints per-sample confidence scores.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
Payload contains one protein molecule and two DNA molecules
assertions.check: Script contains 'type' set to 'protein' and 'type' set to 'dna' in the molecules list

assertions.description: Both DNA sequences appear in the payload
diffusion_samples is set to 2
output_format is set to 'cif'
Script iterates over structures_with_scores to save each sample
assertions.check: Script iterates over the response structures and saves each to a separate .cif file
